title: "[SOLVED] screen resolution stuck at 640x480"
date: 2008-06-11

### Post by Yondergod22 on 2008-06-11
My screen resolution is stuck at 640x480, when I go to system>preferences>screen resolution theres no other options.
heres the result of cat /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Driver		"vesa"
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
EndSection

```

### Post by MmmmJoel on 2008-06-11
Have you installed a restricted driver for your video card? Do so, if one is available.

System -> Administration -> Hardware Drivers

---

### Post by iaculallad on 2008-06-11
You could try this:

Create a backup of your xorg.conf file:

```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup
```

Open xorg.conf file for editing:

```
sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

Select all the text inside the file and delete it, replacing it with the edited version of your xorg.conf file below. Restart.
```

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Driver		"vesa"
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
        HorizSync 30-110
        VertRefresh 50-160
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
        DefaultDepth 24
        SubSection "Display"
        Depth 24
        Modes "1280x1024"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by iaculallad on 2008-06-12
> **Yondergod22 said:**
> nope, there aren't any.
and I don't think the other thing worked, how to I get to the backup

To get back the backup file:

```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup /etc/X11/xorg.conf 
```

if I continue I just get a black screen, and I have to hard reboot. 
if I click configure, I can choose a screen and video card, but none of the ones I tried work, they just went to a black screen.

### Post by iaculallad on 2008-06-12
Try to login via Restore Mode (By pressing ESC key when you seen the "Grub loading...." and issue the command below:

```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```

---

### Post by Yondergod22 on 2008-06-12
grub loads too fast =/ (that is the thing where I choose my OS, right?)

---

### Post by iaculallad on 2008-06-12
> **Yondergod22 said:**
> grub loads too fast =/ (that is the thing where I choose my OS, right?)

Yap. Just press the "ESC" key when you see "Grub loading... " in your screen then select Restore Mode.

---

### Post by Thre365ive on 2008-06-12
I was actually having the same problem, and just fixed it. I just loaded into the Recovery mode and it had an option to restore XServer. I just clicked that, let it run and it worked.

---

### Post by Yondergod22 on 2008-06-12
I only see it for less than a second, and my reaction time isnt very fast. and I tried repeatedly pressing it and it didn't work. I do have an option of booting into recovery mode though, is that the same thing?

---

### Post by Yondergod22 on 2008-06-12
> **Thre365ive said:**
> I was actually having the same problem, and just fixed it. I just loaded into the Recovery mode and it had an option to restore XServer. I just clicked that, let it run and it worked.
OMG it worked! THANKS. and iaculallad, thanks for trying to help

---

### Post by iaculallad on 2008-06-12
> **Yondergod22 said:**
> I only see it for less than a second, and my reaction time isnt very fast. and I tried repeatedly pressing it and it didn't work. I do have an option of booting into recovery mode though, is that the same thing?

Yes, boot using that mode and restore your X.

```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```

---

### Post by iaculallad on 2008-06-12
Follow the steps on the first post I posted on this thread, only this time, copy and paste the edited version of your xorg.conf file below to your xorg.conf file. Save->Close. Restart.

```

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
        HorizSync 30-110
        VertRefresh 50-160
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
        DefaultDepth 24
        SubSection "Display"
        Depth 24
        Modes "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by Binary.tobis on 2008-06-12
When I put the command in the command prompt it said "sudo: gedit: command not found" and when I tried to do it manually, opening, editing and saving the file, it says "Can't open file to write" as I try and save it.

---

### Post by iaculallad on 2008-06-12
> **Binary.tobis said:**
> When I put the command in the command prompt it said "sudo: gedit: command not found" and when I tried to do it manually, opening, editing and saving the file, it says "Can't open file to write" as I try and save it.

Open up your terminal and issue the commands below.

To Backup:

```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup
```

Open xorg.conf for editing:

```
sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

Select all text inside your current xorg.conf file and delete it, replacing it with the edited version below. Save and Close. Restart.

```

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
        HorizSync 30-110
        VertRefresh 50-160
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
        DefaultDepth 24
        SubSection "Display"
        Depth 24
        Modes "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by Binary.tobis on 2008-06-12

### Post by iaculallad on 2008-06-12
> **Binary.tobis said:**
> Thanks for being so helpful, but my Terminal just won't understand the gedit command. ;_; I copied and pasted the command but it says
```
sudo: gedit: command not found

```

Terminal should look similar to this:

> ian@gutsy-gibbon:/$

And this is how you input the command:

ian@gutsy-gibbon:/$ **sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf**

and nothing else...

---

### Post by Binary.tobis on 2008-06-12
Sorry, I'm putting that in and it seems as though I don't even have the gedit command. Here's what I put in:

```
nick@GLaDOS:~$ sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
sudo: gedit: command not found

```

I pressed tab twice and looked through the commands and gedit isn't listed. I'm using xubuntu, would that effect it? (so sorry if it does and I didn't mention it)

---

### Post by iaculallad on 2008-06-12
> **Binary.tobis said:**
> Sorry, I'm putting that in and it seems as though I don't even have the gedit command. Here's what I put in:

```
nick@GLaDOS:~$ sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
sudo: gedit: command not found

```

I pressed tab twice and looked through the commands and gedit isn't listed. I'm using xubuntu, would that effect it? (so sorry if it does and I didn't mention it)

Are you using KDE? Try **kedit** instead.

```
sudo kedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

### Post by sam_delta on 2008-06-12
for xubuntu is mousepad
```
sudo mousepad /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

sam

### Post by verymagicalguy on 2008-06-12
Try mousepad.

```

	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
```

and when I restarted it asked me to log in in a terminal-like screen after the xubuntu loading bar finished, then the xubuntu symbol came back up with a bunch of vertical green lines and locked up. I restarted and pressed escape during the boot up and xfix-ed it, and now it is back to normal with the faulty one-inch border. :( Thanks for trying so far, and grats on post 1000! I will be back tomorrow.
